title: Electronic structure, quasiparticle renormalizations, and magnetic correlations in the alternating single-layer bilayer nickelate La$_5$Ni$_3$O$_{11}$
abstract: Using DFT+DMFT we study the normal-state electronic structure and magnetic correlations of the recently discovered alternating single-layer bilayer Raddlesden-Popper nickelate La$_5$Ni$_3$O$_{11}$ (1212-LNO). Our results exhibit qualitative differences for the structurally distinct single-layer and bilayer Ni ions, implying the importance of confinement and orbital-dependent correlations. The Ni $e_g$ electronic states originating from the bilayer Ni ions form strongly renormalized quasiparticle bands with a large enhancement factor $m^*/m \sim 3.5$ and 4.2 for the Ni $x^2-y^2$ and $3z^2-r^2$ orbitals, respectively. Moreover, the $e_g$ states of the single-layer Ni ions exhibit an orbital-selective Mott insulating state, with a narrow energy gap for the Ni $3z^2-r^2$ states and metallic, strongly incoherent (non-Fermi-liquid) Ni $x^2-y^2$ ones. Our analysis of magnetic correlations suggests the formation of intertwined spin and charge density wave stripes in the bilayer NiO$_6$ slab, in close similarity to the double-layer material. We refine two major instabilities, a leading one is associated with a wave vector ${\bf Q}=(\frac{1}{3},\frac{1}{3})$ (``up-down-0" spin pattern), competing with the bicollinear $(\frac{1}{4},\frac{1}{4})$ (``up-up-down-down") stripe state. The single-layer Ni $3d$ electrons exhibit instability towards the Néel-type magnetic state. Under pressure, 1212-LNO undergoes an orbital-selective Mott insulator-to-metal phase transition, associated with metallization of the single-layer Ni $e_g$ states. As a result, the single-layer Ni $e_g$ bands exhibit non-Fermi-liquid (bad metal) behavior with strongly incoherent spectral weights near $E_F$. We note that correlation effects result in a reconstruction of magnetic correlations as compared to that obtained within DFT. In fact, we observe a crossover from single-layer to double-layer dominated magnetic correlations.
summary: This paper uses advanced DFT+DMFT calculations to study the hybrid nickelate La5Ni3O11. It identifies a unique orbital-selective Mott insulating state in the single-layer Ni ions while the bilayer ions remain metallic but heavily renormalized. The research also shows how electronic correlations reconstruct the magnetic spectrum and how high pressure can drive a transition from an insulator to a metal. These findings are crucial for understanding the interplay between structural complexity and strong electron correlations in new superconducting materials.
main_problem: Understanding the microscopic origins of the anomalous normal-state electronic structure, magnetism, and orbital-dependent correlations in the hybrid nickelate La5Ni3O11 (1212-LNO).
main_result: The study reveals an orbital-selective Mott insulating state in the single-layer Ni ions and strongly renormalized quasiparticle bands in the bilayer Ni ions, with magnetic correlations shifting from single-layer to bilayer-dominated due to correlation effects.
method: Fully charge self-consistent DFT+DMFT using plane-wave pseudopotentials and the continuous-time hybridization expansion quantum Monte Carlo (CTQMC) method.
model_or_system: The physical system is the alternating single-layer/bilayer Ruddlesden-Popper nickelate La5Ni3O11 (1212-LNO), consisting of structurally distinct NiO6 slabs.
key_observables: Quasiparticle renormalization (m*/m), spectral functions, magnetic susceptibility (spin/charge density waves), and local magnetic moments.
important_parameters: Hubbard interaction U = 6 eV, Hund's exchange J = 0.95 eV, and high-pressure regimes (>20 GPa).
main_assumptions: Use of the particle-hole bubble approximation for momentum-resolved static magnetic susceptibility, neglecting higher-order vertex corrections.
paper_structure: The paper investigates the electronic and magnetic properties of 1212-LNO, detailing the orbital-dependent correlations in single-layer vs. bilayer Ni ions, the effects of pressure on the Mott transition, and the reconstruction of magnetic instabilities.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1: k-resolved spectral functions of PM 1212-LNO cal- culated by DFT + DMFT with U = 6 eV and J = 0.95 eV at T = 298 K. The calculations are performed for the low- pressure Cmmm and high-pressure P4/mmm crystal struc- tures using experimental lattice parameters and optimized atomic positions. Our results are compared with the non- magnetic DFT calculations (shown with dashed green lines).

FIG. 4: Orbital-dependent Ni 3d self-energies Im[Σ(iωn)] on the Matsubara axis for the crystallographically distinct Ni sites in the mono- (left) and bilayer NiO6 block (right) for different pressures (shown with symbols). Our results for the Pad´e interpolation of Im[Σ(iωn)] are depicted with lines.

FIG. 2: Our results for the layer-resolved orbital-dependent spectral functions of 1212-LNO at low pressure calculated by DFT + DMFT at T = 298 K. The partial Ni t2g, x2 −y2, and 3z2 −r2 orbital contributions are shown (per orbital). Ni x2 −y2, and 3z2 −r2 orbital states are magnified for better readability.
FIG. 3: Orbital-dependent spectral functions of the high- pressure Fmmm phase of 1212-LNO calculated by DFT + DMFT under pressure ∼30 GPa at T = 298 K.

FIG. 5: Total and orbitally resolved local spin correlation functions χ(τ) = ⟨ˆmz(τ) ˆmz(0)⟩as a function of the imaginary time τ for the Ni 3d orbitals calculated by DFT + DMFT for the low- and high-pressure (at 30 GPa) phase at T = 290 K.
FIG. 6: Correlated Fermi surfaces [spectral function A(k, ω) evaluated at ω = EF ] for kz = 0 (left) and kz = π/c (right) calculated within DFT + DMFT at T = 298 K for different pressures.

FIG. 7: Orbital-dependent in-plane static spin susceptibility χ(q) of PM 1212-LNO for the crystallographically distinct Ni ions in the bilayer and monolayer NiO6 blocks for different pressures obtained by DFT + DMFT at T = 298 K using the particle-hole bubble approximation (neglecting the high-order vertex corrections).

FIG. 9: Orbital-dependent in-plane static spin susceptibility χ(q) of 1212-LNO obtained with the nonmagnetic DFT using the particle-hole bubble approximation (neglecting the high- order vertex corrections).
FIG. 8: Fermi surface cuts of 1212-LNO for kz = 0 (left) and kz = π/c (right) calculated using nonmagnetic DFT for different pressures.
